content:
I can't make ends meet anymore. The rent went up again and I don't know how I'll pay it next month. Every day I wake up anxious about money.

I try to save where I can but it never seems enough.
My landlord hasn't fixed the heating in three months. We're sleeping in coats. It's not right — we're paying full rent for a place that isn't habitable.
I've been working two jobs but still it's not enough. My kids ask for things and I have to say no. That's the hardest part — looking at their faces.
The food bank has been a lifeline. Without it I don't know what we'd do. I try to go early so I don't see my neighbours — it's embarrassing.
I feel ashamed going to the food bank but I have no choice. My pride took a hit but you have to put food on the table. My parents never needed charity.
We moved here last year and I still haven't found regular work. Sometimes the language barrier is overwhelming. I study in the evenings, but progress is slow.
